default: Follow these steps to get your inventory syncing with GunRack.deals.

Your feed must be a publicly accessible URL that returns product data in one of our supported formats: XML, JSON, or CSV. The feed must be reachable by our servers without authentication, or use Basic Auth or an API key.
Your feed must be in CSV, JSON, or XML format. See the examples below each step card for the expected structure.
If your feed uses different field names than our defaults, enter a JSON mapping in the Field Mapping box on the Feed Settings tab. Map your field names to ours.
Go to the Feed Settings tab and enter your feed URL. Select your format (CSV, JSON, or XML). If your feed requires authentication, select the auth type and enter your credentials as JSON.
After your first import completes, go to the Listings tab to see all synced products. Check the Unmatched UPCs tab for any products that couldn't be matched to our catalog.

Feed URL must be publicly accessible or use supported auth
UPC must be a valid 12-digit barcode matching our catalog
Price must be a positive decimal number
Feed must update at least daily
Products must comply with all applicable federal, state, and local laws
NFA items must be clearly identified in your feed
Pricing must reflect your actual selling price
Billing, upgrades, and cancellation are handled through your IPS Commerce customer area. Changes take effect on your next billing cycle.
How to Leave a Helpful Dealer Review

Leaving a review helps other buyers make informed decisions and helps dealers improve. Please keep reviews honest, specific, and based on your actual experience.

What makes a good review:
- Be specific about your transaction — what did you buy, when, how did it go
- Rate each category based on that specific experience
- Pricing Accuracy: did the listed price match what you paid
- Shipping Speed: how fast did the item arrive after ordering
- Customer Service: how responsive and helpful was the dealer

Review rules:
- Reviews must be based on a real transaction with this dealer
- No personal attacks, profanity, or discriminatory language
- Do not include personal information about dealer staff
- One review per dealer — you can update your existing review if your experience changes
- False or fraudulent reviews will be removed

Your review may be contested by the dealer if they believe it is inaccurate. If contested you will be notified by email and have 30 days to respond with supporting evidence.

If a dealer believes a review is inaccurate or fraudulent they may initiate a dispute. Here is how the process works:

Step 1 — Dealer contests the review
The dealer submits a dispute with their reason and any supporting evidence such as order numbers or communications. The review stays visible with an "Under Review" badge while the dispute is active.

Step 2 — You are notified
You will receive an email and site notification explaining the dealer's dispute and their evidence. You have 30 days to respond.

Step 3 — Submit your response
Click the link in your notification to view the dealer's evidence and submit your own response. You can include a written explanation and any supporting evidence you have.

Step 4 — Admin reviews both sides
Once you respond, our admin team reviews both the dealer's evidence and your response and makes a final decision.

Step 5 — Resolution
Admin can uphold the dispute (your review is removed from the dealer's rating average but stays visible), dismiss the dispute (your review stands), or ask you to update your review if the issue has been resolved.

If you do not respond within 30 days the dispute is automatically resolved in the dealer's favor and your review no longer counts toward their rating average.

Note: Dealers have a monthly limit on how many reviews they can dispute to prevent abuse.

As a dealer on GunRack.deals you agree to the following review policy:

- You may not offer incentives in exchange for positive reviews
- You may not ask customers to remove or change legitimate negative reviews
- You may respond publicly to any review — keep responses professional and factual
- You may dispute reviews you believe are fraudulent or factually inaccurate

Dispute limits:
- Basic dealers: 2 disputes per month
- Pro dealers: 5 disputes per month
- Enterprise dealers: unlimited

Abuse of the dispute system — filing disputes on legitimate reviews — may result in dispute privileges being suspended by an administrator. When suspended, you will not be able to file new contests until the suspension is lifted. A suspension notice will appear on your Reviews tab. Contact dealers@gunrack.deals if you believe a suspension was applied in error.

Disputes must include a specific reason and supporting evidence. Vague disputes without evidence will be dismissed by admin.
